meta.title: KT Frontier Elevation and Public Defensibility Campaign
meta.intent: Raise KT from a sealed Advanced closure state into a publicly defensible bounded-SOTA state by eliminating live operational contradictions, restoring delivery integrity, strengthening source/build trust, packaging detached verification, enabling independent reproduction, and opening exactly one bounded public horizon with machine-checkable proof.
teaching_guidance: Make KT less mystical and more undeniable.
Do not add ornamental architecture while live proof surfaces are still dirty.
Do not solve three things halfway; solve one thing completely.
Prefer repair of known red lanes over invention of new subsystems.
Any claim not forced by receipts is forbidden.
Any residual class must be typed, bounded, and named; never hand-wave.

current_invariants_already_won: governance claim narrowing is sealed and explicit
known_live_grade_caps_from_rerun_audit: operator-factory readiness held by failing status/ci_sim lanes
authority grading capped by stale or missing canonical HMAC delivery-integrity path
audit target not pristine while local residue or secret-like files remain
no open public horizon with replayable receipts

mission.primary: Convert KT from a sealed internal closure stack into a stronger public proof appliance with cleaner live operator reality and one bounded open proving ground.
secondary: Eliminate every audit-grade defect that is currently fixable without lying about the remaining ceiling.
Make source, build, publication, and verification stronger than repo-local trust.
Ensure live readiness and authority grades converge upward toward the sealed closure state instead of contradicting it.
Preserve lawful narrowing where proof remains absent.
non_goals_until_explicitly_unlocked: No beyond-SOTA-overall claim while red critical lanes remain.
No platform-governance inflation beyond WORKFLOW_GOVERNANCE_ONLY unless admissible proof exists.
No architectural sprawl unrelated to measurable closure pressure.

program_objective.target_audit_state: Bounded system moves from Advanced/A- toward SOTA/A or A+ on the live audited target.
success_is: one bounded public horizon open with receipts

design_alignment: SLSA source/build provenance and verification-summary posture

kt_translation.root_of_trust: attested subject artifacts plus verifier trust policy
kt_translation.ledger_role: published witness plane and live truth pointer
kt_translation.repo_role: canonical active tree plus documentary evidence surfaces
kt_translation.proof_appliance_goal: any serious auditor can verify KT from detached tooling and published evidence without tribal knowledge

operator_doctrine.micro_step_rule: within each workstream, execute only the next unresolved atomic step; do not parallelize unresolved defect classes
operator_doctrine.mandatory_pause_rule: after each workstream seal, stop and wait for explicit approval string APPROVED: PROCEED
pre_mutation_actions: rescan all relevant files/receipts in chronological order
audit full blast radius across touched and downstream-affected surfaces
confirm current claim ceiling and active blockers
record baseline commands, outputs, failing tests, and expected post-fix deltas
mutation_discipline: touch only what the workstream requires
do not silently reclassify historical or documentary surfaces into active truth
do not widen a workstream to absorb adjacent cleanup unless the adjacent cleanup is a declared blocker to the current step
prefer deletion, demotion, or explicit quarantine over vague compatibility hacks
proof_discipline: every material change must emit a receipt
every receipt must name subject head and evidence head
every pass verdict must be machine-checkable
every blocker must be named, typed, and preserved until actually resolved
autonomy_limits: the bot may work autonomously inside the current workstream only
the bot may not self-authorize the next workstream
the bot may not upgrade proof class, readiness, governance, or public horizon state without the required receipts
the bot may not omit failing evidence to make a workstream look clean

global_invariants: No claim may outrun computed proof class.
No active truth may be read from documentary mirrors.
No workstream may silently reopen archive contamination.
No workstream may silently weaken canonical runner determinism.
No workstream may silently weaken verifier independence or publication attestation.
No workstream may silently weaken lawful governance narrowing.
No secret-like file may remain in the audited target.
No workstream may seal while git status is dirty.
No public-horizon opening is lawful without replayable receipts.
forbidden_moves: Do not claim platform-enforced governance while branch-protection proof is blocked.
Do not claim current HEAD itself is the transparency-verified subject when head_equals_subject is false.
Do not claim H1 is open until a dedicated horizon receipt says so.
Do not invent a detached verifier while it still depends on hidden repo-local state.
Do not call a build hermetic unless inputs, environment, and outputs are actually pinned and reproduced.
Do not count documentary mentions as runtime defects unless they are execution-coupled.
Do not widen public claims because the claim compiler text looks strong.

cleanliness_and_waste_control.policy: The bot must minimize artifact sprawl and may not solve the defect by increasing unmanaged artifact count without explicit justification.
no_leftover_classes: one-off migration utilities without explicit retention justification
generated artifacts outside approved output locations
stale receipts superseded by the current workstream unless policy requires retention
cleanliness_and_waste_control.temporary_utility_rule: Any file classified as temporary migration utility must be deleted before sealing unless the workstream explicitly ratifies it as permanent.
cleanliness_and_waste_control.generated_artifact_rule: Any generated artifact must be written only to approved artifact locations.
cleanliness_and_waste_control.superseded_file_rule: Any superseded file must be removed, demoted to documentary or historical, or explicitly retained with a stated reason in the receipt.
preferred_resolution_order: add new surface only if no lawful existing surface can carry the fix

pass_verdict_rules: must not imply higher readiness or legitimacy than actually proven

hard_stop_if_true: critical CI remains red after recovery workstream
delivery-integrity evidence remains missing after restoration workstream
detached verifier still depends on hidden local state
no bounded public horizon is actually opened
live grades still materially contradict closure receipts at campaign end

workstreams.title: Repo Hygiene and Audit Target Cleanroom
workstreams.intent: Eliminate all noncanonical local residue and bring the audited target to a clean, secrets-free, commit-ready state.
workstreams.why_now: A dirty worktree, secret residue, or shadow files will poison every later audit, attestation, and public claim.
tasks: inventory all secret-like files, local-only files, and untracked residue
remove or quarantine .env.secret and any equivalent secret-bearing artifact from the audited target
commit or remove restored audit packets and any other drift that changes the audit surface
ensure ignored files do not hide runtime-significant dependencies
emit clean-state receipt only after git status is clean
pass_conditions: No secret-like file remains in the audited target.
No untracked or uncommitted file changes the audit surface.
git status is clean.
Ignored-file policy does not conceal runtime-significant inputs.
fail_conditions: Any secret-like file remains.
Any untracked artifact changes the audit target.
The workstream mutates protected runtime or authority surfaces.
The repo is not clean at seal time.

workstreams.title: Operator-Factory Greenline Recovery
workstreams.intent: Repair the failing live operator lanes so readiness is no longer held down by red status and ci_sim failures.
workstreams.why_now: No SOTA claim survives red critical CI.
allowed_surface_classes: truth-publication tests tied to failing lanes
forbidden_surface_classes: historical/doc-only surfaces except as test fixtures
tasks: baseline every currently failing lane and preserve the failing evidence
repair status lane
repair certify ci_sim lane
repair failing law-bundle, operator CLI, deterministic report, hashpin, and truth-publication surfaces if they are part of the live red set
re-run readiness grading on the repaired target
emit exact before/after failure delta matrix
pass_conditions: status lane passes
certify ci_sim passes
all prior critical red failures targeted by this workstream are green
fresh readiness grade no longer holds on those exact defect classes
fail_conditions: Any targeted critical lane remains red
A fix weakens canonical runner or authority proofs
Readiness remains HOLD for the same unresolved classes

workstreams.title: Delivery Integrity Restoration
workstreams.intent: Restore the canonical HMAC delivery pack path and clear stale delivery-integrity caps on authority grading.
workstreams.why_now: Authority cannot stay strong while delivery-integrity evidence is stale or missing.
tasks: identify the canonical delivery pack path expected by the authority grader
recreate or correctly repoint the canonical HMAC delivery pack
validate manifest completeness and integrity against current authority receipts
rerun authority grading on the repaired target
record exact before/after grade impact
pass_conditions: Canonical delivery pack path exists and is valid.
Authority-grade inputs resolve cleanly.
Authority grade no longer holds on missing or stale delivery-integrity evidence.
fail_conditions: Delivery path remains missing or stale.
Authority grading remains capped by the same delivery defect.
Repair unlawfully mutates archive or documentary-only surfaces.

workstreams.title: Hermetic Build Envelope
workstreams.intent: Define and verify a hermetic or near-hermetic build envelope for the detached verifier and critical publication bundle set.
workstreams.why_now: Build verification is stronger when the environment is pinned before provenance/VSA are emitted.
tasks: declare the critical artifact set to be built hermetically
pin critical build inputs and remove hidden network dependence where practical
declare reproducibility-critical environment variables and strip noncritical wall-clock variation
rebuild in the declared envelope and compare subject hashes
emit exact envelope manifest and rebuild receipt
pass_conditions: Critical build environment is declaratively specified.
Critical build inputs are pinned.
Rebuild in the declared envelope reproduces the expected subject artifacts for the declared set.
fail_conditions: Hidden network or environment dependencies remain in the critical build path.
Hermetic claim is made for surfaces not actually pinned and reproduced.
Rebuild does not match expected subjects.

workstreams.title: Source Trust and Revision Attestation
workstreams.intent: Add source-side trust evidence so KT is revision-attested as well as publication-attested.
workstreams.why_now: Current KT is strong on publication proof; source/revision trust is the next leverage point.
tasks: define protected revision surfaces
require signing of release-relevant commits and tags
emit source provenance for the evaluated revision
bind revision trust policy to later verifier consumption
pass_conditions: Protected release revisions are signed or explicitly policy-exempt with justification.
Source provenance exists for the evaluated revision.
Revision trust can be tied to later build/publication verification.
fail_conditions: Unsigned protected release revisions remain admissible.
Source provenance cannot be tied to the evaluated revision.

workstreams.title: Build Provenance and Verification Summary Upgrade
workstreams.intent: Upgrade KT from publication attestation only to stronger build-verification posture with explicit provenance and verification-summary outputs.
workstreams.why_now: Once the build envelope is defined, provenance and verification summary become much stronger evidence instead of decorative metadata.
tasks: emit build provenance for the critical artifact set
emit verification summary attestation for the verified artifact set
bind provenance, verification summary, and publication receipts to the same artifact subjects
upgrade verifier outputs to reference these stronger build-side proofs
pass_conditions: Build provenance exists for canonical release artifacts.
Verification Summary Attestation exists for the verified artifact set.
Verifier outputs can cite both provenance and verification summary for the same artifact subjects.
fail_conditions: Artifact subjects do not match across provenance, verification summary, and publication receipts.
Attestations exist but are not verified by policy.

workstreams.title: Detached Public Verifier Package
workstreams.intent: Turn the public verifier into a detached proof appliance that can run outside the repo checkout.
workstreams.why_now: Repo-local verification is strong; detached verification is harder to dispute.
tasks: package the verifier for detached execution
ensure it resolves trust root, provenance, Rekor, and authority state without hidden repo-local assumptions
sign the detached verifier release and emit its SBOM
compare detached verifier conclusion to repo-local verifier conclusion
pass_conditions: Verifier package runs detached from the repo checkout.
Detached verifier checks trust root, provenance, Rekor, and authority state correctly.
Detached verifier conclusion matches repo-local verifier conclusion.
fail_conditions: Detached verifier still depends on hidden repo-local files or mutable local state.
Detached verifier and repo-local verifier disagree.

workstreams.title: Independent External Reproduction
workstreams.intent: Prove KT can be independently reproduced or verified from outside the original operator environment.
workstreams.why_now: Detached tooling without outside reproduction is still too self-contained to be maximally persuasive.
tasks: define at least two independent clean environments
reproduce the critical artifact set or verify the attested subjects in both
record environment metadata and exact replay instructions
emit a replay recipe that does not rely on tribal knowledge
pass_conditions: At least two independent clean environments reproduce or successfully verify the declared critical artifact set.
Results are recorded with environment metadata.
External recipe is end-to-end and explicit.
fail_conditions: Only the original operator environment can reproduce or verify the results.
External recipe relies on tribal knowledge or hidden state.

workstreams.title: Bounded Public Horizon Open
workstreams.intent: Open exactly one bounded public horizon so KT is no longer purely internally adjudicated.
workstreams.why_now: This is the step that kills the 'still blocked / still internal' critique.
forbidden_surface_classes: broad public or tournament claims outside the chosen horizon
tasks: select one horizon only
define exact scope, inputs, outputs, and replay contract
open the chosen horizon with receipts and bounded claim language
prove the horizon is externally inspectable or replayable
pass_conditions: Exactly one horizon is opened.
The opened horizon has scope contract, blocker matrix, and pass/fail receipts.
The opened horizon is externally inspectable or replayable.
fail_conditions: A horizon is marked open by narrative rather than receipts.
Multiple horizons are silently widened.
Scope is vague or not replayable.

workstreams.title: Independent Red Team and Bounty Bootstrap
workstreams.intent: Invite non-author reviewers to attack the proof plane and record results in the evidence plane.
workstreams.why_now: External challenge data is stronger than self-congratulation.
forbidden_surface_classes: burying challenge results outside evidence surfaces
tasks: define exact 'kill' conditions for the red team
publish challenge protocol and submission format
record at least one externally runnable red-team or auditor path
require all findings to be dispositioned in receipts
pass_conditions: Independent challenge protocol exists.
At least one external red-team or auditor recipe is runnable.
Findings are logged and dispositioned without weakening claim discipline.
fail_conditions: Red-team process is symbolic and not executable.
Findings can be buried or resolved narratively.

workstreams.title: Formal Invariant Proof Core
workstreams.intent: Formally specify and check the small set of invariants that matter most to KT's claim boundary.
workstreams.why_now: Only a few invariants truly matter for KT's legitimacy; those should be modeled, not merely tested.
forbidden_surface_classes: toy invariants unrelated to release-critical logic
tasks: formally model the three required invariants
run the model checker
record results and any assumptions or bounded abstractions
pass_conditions: All three required invariants are modeled.
Model-check results are recorded.
Modeled invariants correspond to real release-critical logic.
fail_conditions: Formal methods are claimed broadly but only toy examples exist.
Modeled invariants do not correspond to real release-critical logic.

workstreams.title: Frontier Recut and SOTA Readjudication
workstreams.intent: Rerun the adversarial audit on the cleaned, externally reproducible, publicly opened state and decide whether KT has actually crossed into bounded SOTA on the live audited target.
workstreams.why_now: This is the only lawful place to make a stronger system-wide claim.
tasks: rerun full adversarial audit on the final audited target
compare March baseline, WS12 state, and frontier-elevated state
check that live readiness and authority grades no longer materially contradict closure receipts
issue bounded final verdict only from current evidence
pass_conditions: All prior receipts resolve on the final audited target.
Live readiness and authority grades no longer materially undercut the closure claim.
The rerun audit compares baseline, WS12, and final state explicitly.
fail_conditions: Audit packet is stale or irreproducible.
Live grades still materially contradict the claimed frontier posture.

allowed_end_states: SOTA on a bounded system with one open public horizon and no red critical live-grade contradictions.
Advanced-plus with honest remaining blockers if one or more campaign gates fail.
forbidden_end_states: GOD_TIER claim with blocked public horizons.
Beyond-SOTA overall claim with red critical CI or missing delivery integrity.
Public-horizon claims that are not externally replayable.
